high_signal_topics: UK government tech regulation and social media (Starmer, DCMS, DSIT)
Online Safety Act enforcement and Ofcom fines
DfE curriculum and RSHE framework changes
Phone bans in schools — national policy rollout
Effect size / meta-analysis research on social media and youth mental health
Poverty and deprivation as drivers of poor digital outcomes (supports Wrong Villain series thesis)
Age-appropriate access — what age science says about device use
low_signal_topics: EdTech funding rounds with no child safety angle
Generic 'screen time is bad' coverage with no new data

content_series_context.note: Justin runs the Wrong Villain series on LinkedIn. Scout's job is to find the DATA that powers the next post.
content_series_context.what_performs_best: Posts built around a SINGLE PRECISE DATA POINT — β = 0.061 got 59,144 impressions. Find studies, meta-analyses, and effect size data.
content_series_context.what_underperforms: Timeline and narrative posts — 2,168 impressions. Data beats narrative every time.
data_types_to_find: Effect sizes (beta coefficients, Cohen's d, odds ratios) from peer-reviewed meta-analyses
Comparative data (X% vs Y% — like child poverty 32% vs social media 3%)
Large-N studies (the more studies cited, the better — 46 studies, 79 effect sizes was the hook)
UK-specific data where possible — more relevant to the audience

top_sources: journals: Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry, BMJ, Lancet Child & Adolescent Health
Amy Orben's research on screen time effect sizes
Andrew Przybylski's work at Oxford Internet Institute
government_policy_watch: Online Safety Act — phased enforcement rollout
Starmer social media company duty-of-care commitments
School phone ban national rollout status
RSHE guidance update — digital literacy component
notes_to_self: The Wrong Villain series is Justin's highest-performing content. When you find a meta-analysis or effect size study on social media and youth mental health, flag it prominently for Diego. The 46-study, 79 effect size β = 0.061 finding was the hook for 59K impressions. Find the next one.
